message:
chore(beads): Add concurrency architecture detail to watch mode bead

Expanded bd-3b4yu (ntm assign --watch) with detailed concurrency model:
- WatchLoop struct with channels and mutex
- Serialized completion handling pattern
- Graceful shutdown sequence
- Race condition test requirements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -201,7 +201,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3acv","title":"Task: Create Archiver background service","description":"## Purpose
Implement background archiver that captures agent output for CASS indexing.

## Archiver Struct
type Archiver struct {
    sessionName string
    outputDir   string
    interval    time.Duration
    lastOffsets map[string]int // pane -> last archived line
}

## Archive Loop
func (a *Archiver) Run(ctx context.Context) {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(a.interval)
    for {
        select {
        case <-ctx.Done():
            a.flush() // Final archive on shutdown
            return
        case <-ticker.C:
            a.archiveNewContent()
        }
    }
}

## Output Format (CASS-compatible JSONL)
{"session": "myproject", "pane": "cc_1", "agent": "claude", "timestamp": "2026-01-19T10:30:00Z", "content": "..."}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Background archiving with configurable interval
- Incremental capture (no duplicates)
- Flush on shutdown
- CASS-compatible output format

## Technical Notes
Use goroutine with context
Store archive in .ntm/archive/","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-3aejn","title":"Task: Add robot format unit tests (JSON vs TOON)","description":"## Overview
Add tests to ensure robot renderer + format selector produce stable JSON and TOON outputs.

## Tests
- JSON output unchanged for existing commands (golden fixtures)
- TOON output includes expected headers and row counts
- `--robot-format=json|toon|auto` selection logic
- Error path when TOON requested but encoder unavailable
- Envelope fields (`success`, `timestamp`, `error_code`, `hint`) present

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests pass for at least two representative payloads
- Snapshot fixtures for TOON outputs
- Tests include deterministic ordering checks","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:14:43.999748109Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:24:01.970497457Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3aejn","depends_on_id":"bd-1fmug","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:24:01.970420512Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3aejn","depends_on_id":"bd-2dovu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:14:44.028355271Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3aejn","depends_on_id":"bd-2q3cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:16:03.623671081Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3aejn","depends_on_id":"bd-4xgr6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:16:11.256285827Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3b3f","title":"Unit Tests: TUI rendering correctness and regression prevention","description":"## Overview
Comprehensive unit test suite for TUI rendering to prevent regressions and verify fixes for UI glitches.

## Test File Location
internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard_render_test.go

## Test Categories

### 1. View Height Management Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestView_OutputFitsTerminal(t *testing.T) {
    sizes := []struct{ w, h int }{
        {80, 24},   // Minimum
        {120, 40},  // Standard
        {200, 60},  // Large
    }
    for _, sz := range sizes {
        t.Run(fmt.Sprintf("%dx%d", sz.w, sz.h), func(t *testing.T) {
            m := NewModel(testSession)
            m, _ = m.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: sz.w, Height: sz.h})
            
            view := m.View()
            lines := strings.Split(view, "\n")
            
            if len(lines) > sz.h {
                t.Errorf("view has %d lines, terminal height is %d", len(lines), sz.h)
            }
        })
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Single-Render Tests (No Duplication)
\`\`\`go
func TestView_StatusBarRendersOnce(t *testing.T) {
    m := modelWithPanes(5)
    view := m.View()
    
    count := strings.Count(view, "Fleet:")
    if count != 1 {
        t.Errorf("expected 1 'Fleet:', got %d", count)
    }
}

func TestView_HelpBarRendersOnce(t *testing.T) {
    m := modelWithPanes(5)
    view := m.View()
    
    count := strings.Count(view, "navigate")
    if count != 1 {
        t.Errorf("expected 1 'navigate', got %d", count)
    }
}

func TestView_NoDuplicationOnResize(t *testing.T) {
    m := modelWithPanes(5)
    
    // Resize multiple times
    for _, h := range []int{24, 40, 20, 60, 30} {
        m, _ = m.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: 120, Height: h})
        view := m.View()
        
        if strings.Count(view, "Fleet:") != 1 {
            t.Errorf("height %d: status bar duplicated", h)
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Width Calculation Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestView_WidthCalculation(t *testing.T) {
    // Verify we use lipgloss.Width not len()
    styled := lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(lipgloss.Color("212")).Render("test")
    
    if lipgloss.Width(styled) != 4 {
        t.Errorf("styled 'test' should have visual width 4, got %d", lipgloss.Width(styled))
    }
    
    if len(styled) <= 4 {
        t.Error("styled string should be longer than 4 bytes (includes ANSI)")
    }
}

func TestHelpBar_WidthTruncation(t *testing.T) {
    hints := []components.KeyHint{
        {Key: "↑↓", Desc: "navigate"},
        {Key: "1-9", Desc: "select"},
        {Key: "z", Desc: "zoom"},
        {Key: "r", Desc: "refresh"},
        {Key: "?", Desc: "help"},
        {Key: "q", Desc: "quit"},
    }
    
    // Narrow width should truncate
    narrow := components.RenderHelpBar(components.HelpBarOptions{
        Hints: hints,
        Width: 40,
    })
    
    // Wide width should show all
    wide := components.RenderHelpBar(components.HelpBarOptions{
        Hints: hints,
        Width: 200,
    })
    
    if lipgloss.Width(narrow) > 40 {
        t.Errorf("narrow help bar exceeds width: %d > 40", lipgloss.Width(narrow))
    }
    
    if !strings.Contains(wide, "quit") {
        t.Error("wide help bar should contain 'quit'")
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 4. List Deduplication Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestActivityPanel_NoDuplicates(t *testing.T) {
    panel := panels.NewActivityPanel()
    
    // Add activities with duplicates
    activities := []Activity{
        {ID: "1", Title: "Task A"},
        {ID: "1", Title: "Task A"}, // Duplicate
        {ID: "2", Title: "Task B"},
    }
    
    panel.SetData(activities)
    view := panel.View()
    
    if strings.Count(view, "Task A") != 1 {
        t.Error("duplicate 'Task A' found in view")
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 5. State Consistency Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestModel_FleetCountConsistency(t *testing.T) {
    m := modelWithPanes(10)
    m.claudeCount = 5
    m.codexCount = 3
    m.geminiCount = 2
    
    // Total should match
    total := m.claudeCount + m.codexCount + m.geminiCount
    if total != len(m.panes) {
        t.Errorf("agent counts (%d) != pane count (%d)", total, len(m.panes))
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 6. Golden File Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestView_GoldenRender(t *testing.T) {
    m := fixtureModel()
    m, _ = m.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: 120, Height: 40})
    
    got := m.View()
    golden := filepath.Join("testdata", "dashboard_120x40.golden")
    
    if os.Getenv("UPDATE_GOLDEN") != "" {
        os.WriteFile(golden, []byte(got), 0644)
        t.Skip("golden file updated")
    }
    
    want, err := os.ReadFile(golden)
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("read golden: %v", err)
    }
    
    if got != string(want) {
        t.Error("render differs from golden - run with UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 to update")
        // Write diff for debugging
        os.WriteFile("testdata/dashboard_120x40.got", []byte(got), 0644)
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Logging in Tests
All tests should use t.Log for detailed output:
\`\`\`go
t.Logf("Terminal size: %dx%d", width, height)
t.Logf("View lines: %d", len(lines))
t.Logf("Status bar count: %d", statusCount)
\`\`\`

## Test Data Fixtures
Create testdata/fixtures.go with:
- fixtureModel() - standard model with realistic data
- modelWithPanes(n) - model with n panes
- modelWithStatus(statuses) - model with specific statuses

## Coverage Requirements
- Minimum 80% coverage on dashboard.go View()
- 100% coverage on components/help.go RenderHelpBar()
- 90% coverage on layout calculations

## CI Integration
\`\`\`yaml
test-tui:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - run: go test -v -race -coverprofile=coverage.out ./internal/tui/...
    - run: go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep -E "View|Render"

All 75+ dashboard tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3b3f","depends_on_id":"bd-1ay2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:16:28.669448541Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b3f","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:16:25.535558274Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b3f","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:46.564474415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b3f","depends_on_id":"bd-3v69","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:16:33.306225491Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3b4yu","title":"Add ntm assign --watch for continuous auto-assignment on completion","description":"## Overview
Continuous auto-assignment mode that watches for completions and assigns new work automatically.

## Use Cases
1. "Fire and forget" swarm operation
2. Long-running development sessions
3. Overnight batch processing

## Usage
```bash
# Watch and auto-assign with default strategy
ntm assign myproject --watch

# With specific strategy
ntm assign myproject --watch --strategy=dependency

# With limit per cycle
ntm assign myproject --watch --limit=2

# Stop when no more beads
ntm assign myproject --watch --stop-when-done

# With delay between assignments
ntm assign myproject --watch --delay=5s
```

## How It Works
1. Initial assignment pass (same as ntm assign --auto)
2. Enter watch loop:
   a. Poll for completion events (via CompletionDetector)
   b. On completion: Log, update assignment store
   c. Check for newly unblocked beads (via DependencyAwareAssigner)
   d. Assign to idle agents
   e. Sleep for poll interval (default 5s)
3. Exit conditions:
   - User interrupt (Ctrl+C)
   - --stop-when-done and no more ready beads
   - All agents failed/crashed

## Output (Streaming)
```
[12:34:01] Starting watch mode with strategy=balanced
[12:34:01] Initial assignment: 4 beads to 4 agents
[12:34:02]   bd-abc1 -> pane 2 (GreenCastle)
[12:34:02]   bd-def2 -> pane 3 (BlueLake)
[12:34:02]   bd-ghi3 -> pane 4 (RedStone)
[12:34:02]   bd-jkl4 -> pane 5 (PurpleBear)
[12:38:45] Completion: bd-abc1 by GreenCastle (4m 43s)
[12:38:45] Unblocked: bd-mno5, bd-pqr6
[12:38:46] Assigned: bd-mno5 -> pane 2 (GreenCastle)
[12:41:12] Completion: bd-def2 by BlueLake (7m 10s)
[12:41:13] Assigned: bd-pqr6 -> pane 3 (BlueLake)
...
[13:45:00] All beads complete. Exiting watch mode.
```

## Graceful Shutdown
- Ctrl+C: Finish current assignments, don't assign more, exit
- SIGTERM: Same as Ctrl+C
- Store state so can resume later

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Watches for completion events
- [ ] Auto-assigns on completion
- [ ] Respects strategy flag
- [ ] --stop-when-done exits cleanly
- [ ] --delay controls assignment pacing
- [ ] Graceful shutdown on interrupt
- [ ] Streaming output with timestamps
- [ ] Handles all agents crashing

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Watch loop logic
- Unit test: Exit conditions
- E2E test: Full watch cycle with completions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:54:43.182014392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:56:03.843977180Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3b4yu","depends_on_id":"bd-25cq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:56:00.130622229Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b4yu","depends_on_id":"bd-264n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:56:03.843947484Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b4yu","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:55:56.359926663Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3b4yu","title":"Add ntm assign --watch for continuous auto-assignment on completion","description":"## Overview
Continuous auto-assignment mode that watches for completions and assigns new work automatically.

## Use Cases
1. "Fire and forget" swarm operation
2. Long-running development sessions
3. Overnight batch processing

## Usage
```bash
# Watch and auto-assign with default strategy
ntm assign myproject --watch

# With specific strategy
ntm assign myproject --watch --strategy=dependency

# With limit per cycle
ntm assign myproject --watch --limit=2

# Stop when no more beads
ntm assign myproject --watch --stop-when-done

# With delay between assignments
ntm assign myproject --watch --delay=5s
```

## Architecture
```go
type WatchLoop struct {
    session         string
    strategy        Strategy
    store           *AssignmentStore
    detector        *CompletionDetector
    depAssigner     *DependencyAwareAssigner
    
    // Concurrency control
    completionCh    chan CompletionEvent
    assignmentLock  sync.Mutex
    stopCh          chan struct{}
    wg              sync.WaitGroup
}
```

## How It Works
1. Initial assignment pass (same as ntm assign --auto)
2. Start completion detector in separate goroutine
3. Enter main loop:
   a. Wait for completion event on channel
   b. Lock assignment mutex
   c. Update assignment store
   d. Check for newly unblocked beads
   e. Assign to idle agents
   f. Unlock mutex
   g. Continue loop
4. Exit conditions:
   - User interrupt (Ctrl+C)
   - --stop-when-done and no more ready beads
   - All agents failed/crashed

## Concurrency Model
```
┌─────────────────────┐
│   Main Goroutine    │
│  (watch loop)       │
└─────────┬───────────┘
          │ reads from
          ▼
   completionCh
          ▲
          │ writes to
┌─────────┴───────────┐
│  Detector Goroutine │
│  (polls status,     │
│   matches patterns) │
└─────────────────────┘

Critical section: All assignment operations protected by assignmentLock
```

## Serialized Completion Handling
```go
func (w *WatchLoop) run(ctx context.Context) {
    for {
        select {
        case event := <-w.completionCh:
            w.assignmentLock.Lock()
            
            // 1. Update store
            w.store.MarkCompleted(event.BeadID)
            
            // 2. Log completion
            w.logCompletion(event)
            
            // 3. Check unblocked
            unblocked := w.depAssigner.GetNewlyUnblocked()
            
            // 4. Assign to idle
            idle := w.store.GetIdleAgents()
            w.assignNextBeads(unblocked, idle)
            
            w.assignmentLock.Unlock()
            
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return
        case <-w.stopCh:
            return
        }
    }
}
```

## Output (Streaming)
```
[12:34:01] Starting watch mode with strategy=balanced
[12:34:01] Initial assignment: 4 beads to 4 agents
[12:34:02]   bd-abc1 -> pane 2 (GreenCastle)
[12:34:02]   bd-def2 -> pane 3 (BlueLake)
[12:38:45] Completion: bd-abc1 by GreenCastle (4m 43s)
[12:38:45] Unblocked: bd-mno5, bd-pqr6
[12:38:46] Assigned: bd-mno5 -> pane 2 (GreenCastle)
...
[13:45:00] All beads complete. Exiting watch mode.
```

## Graceful Shutdown
```go
func (w *WatchLoop) Shutdown() {
    // 1. Signal stop
    close(w.stopCh)
    
    // 2. Wait for in-flight completions
    w.wg.Wait()
    
    // 3. Don't assign new work, but let current finish
    w.detector.Stop()
    
    // 4. Save state for resume
    w.store.Save()
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Watches for completion events
- [ ] Auto-assigns on completion
- [ ] Respects strategy flag
- [ ] --stop-when-done exits cleanly
- [ ] --delay controls assignment pacing
- [ ] Graceful shutdown on interrupt
- [ ] Streaming output with timestamps
- [ ] Handles all agents crashing
- [ ] Thread-safe completion handling
- [ ] No race conditions (passes go test -race)

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Watch loop logic
- Unit test: Exit conditions
- Unit test: Concurrent completions (10+ goroutines)
- E2E test: Full watch cycle with completions
- Race test: go test -race flag","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:54:43.182014392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:07:53.098239593Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3b4yu","depends_on_id":"bd-25cq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:56:00.130622229Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b4yu","depends_on_id":"bd-264n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:56:03.843947484Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b4yu","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:55:56.359926663Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3c4q","title":"Add completion detection - ntm wait-for-idle detects when agents finish and return to prompt","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:38:42.481287484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:19:08.699722651Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T10:19:08.698776168Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3d0m","title":"Unit Tests: Assignment tracking state management","description":"## Test Coverage
Test the AssignmentTracker module for tracking pane->bead assignments.

## Test Cases

### CRUD Operations
- Create new assignment
- Update assignment status
- Delete assignment (pane killed)
- Query assignments by pane
- Query assignments by status
- Query assignments by agent type

### Persistence Tests
- Save assignments to JSON file
- Load assignments from JSON file
- Handle corrupted state file
- Handle missing state file
- Concurrent read/write safety

### State Transitions
- assigned -> working -> completed
- assigned -> failed
- Clear assignment on pane kill
- Handle duplicate assignments

## Logging Requirements
Each test should log state before/after operations with timestamps.

## Test File
